2606.02867.title: The Epi-LLM Framework: probing LLM behavioral priors through epidemiological agent-based models
2606.02867.paper_summary: Human behaviour during epidemics affects infectious disease dynamics, but quantifying this remains deeply challenging. Here we introduce the Epi-LLM framework: a novel integration of agent-based modelling, real-life epigames, and large language models (LLMs) in which a synthetic society of agents reasons and adapts dynamically over an outbreak contact network. Comparing synthetic agent behaviour against a no-intervention SEIR baseline and human participant data from the AUIB epigame study, we find that LLM agents across four different architectures reduced peak active infections, with quarantine compliance peaking at 58-65% on day six of the 15-day simulation. A binomial generalised linear model showed that perceived health severity was the strongest predictor of quarantine behaviour ($β= 0.33, p = 0.002$), yielding a pseudo-$R^2$ of 0.055, comparable to the 0.072 observed in the human trial. LLM architecture is a key determinant of epidemic dynamics: low-variance architectures offer greater internal validity for testing behavioural rules, while high-variance models may better represent real-world decision-making. Geographic labels alone do not induce culturally differentiated behaviour; explicit attitudinal parameterisation is required. This proof-of-principle work lays the groundwork for deploying the Epi-LLM framework as a scalable, risk-free simulation environment for pandemic preparedness research.
2606.02867.paper_summary_zh: 人類在疫情期間的行為影響傳染病的動態，但量化這一點仍然非常具有挑戰性。<lb/>在這裡，我們介紹了Epi-LLM框架：這是一種新穎的整合，結合了基於代理的建模、現實生活的流行病遊戲和大型語言模型（LLMs），在這個框架中，合成社會的代理在疫情接觸網絡上進行推理和動態適應。<lb/>通過將合成代理的行為與無干預的SEIR基線以及來自AUIB流行病遊戲研究的人類參與者數據進行比較，我們發現四種不同架構的LLM代理減少了峰值活躍感染，隔離遵從率在15天模擬的第六天達到58-65%的高峰。<lb/>一個二項式廣義線性模型顯示，感知的健康嚴重性是隔離行為的最強預測因子（$β= 0.33, p = 0.002$），產生的伪-$R^2$為0.055，與人類試驗中觀察到的0.072相當。<lb/>LLM架構是疫情動態的關鍵決定因素：低方差架構提供了更大的內部有效性來測試行為規則，而高方差模型可能更好地代表現實世界的決策。<lb/>僅僅地理標籤並不會引發文化上差異化的行為；需要明確的態度參數化。<lb/>這項原理驗證工作為部署Epi-LLM框架作為可擴展、無風險的模擬環境以進行疫情準備研究奠定了基礎。
2606.02867.author: Petra Ferenz et.al.
2606.02867.authors: Petra Ferenz, Ava Keeling, Tobias O'Keefe, Lorenzo Stigliano, Francesco Di Lauro, Andres Colubri, Jasmina Panovska-Griffiths
